title: Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards: government response to report
description: The government's response to the Committee's 2020 report Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards
summary: The government has responded to a 2020 report on artificial intelligence and public standards by outlining its plans to address related issues. The specific measures detailed in the response are based on the report's recommendations and aims to shape the development of AI in line with societal values.

title: Report and impact assessment on Copyright and Artificial Intelligence
description: A report and impact assessment on the use of copyright works in the development of artificial intelligence (AI) systems.
summary: This report assesses the impact of using copyrighted materials in the development of artificial intelligence systems. It explores the potential effects of copyright laws on AI systems and their applications.

title: Generative artificial intelligence (AI) in education
description: The Department for Education’s (DfE) position on the use of generative AI in the education sector.
summary: The Department for Education's policy focuses on the responsible use of generative AI in education, aiming to balance its potential benefits with risks and concerns. This approach likely encompasses guidelines or regulations for teachers, students, and institutions to ensure safe and effective integration of generative AI in educational settings.

title: Transport artificial intelligence action plan
description: Sets out how DfT will work with the transport sector to exploit the opportunities from AI while managing potential risks.
summary: This document outlines a plan for using artificial intelligence in transportation. It aims to balance the benefits and risks of AI in the transport sector.

title: Copyright and artificial intelligence: progress report
description: A progress report on the use of copyright works in the development of AI systems, under Section 137 of the Data (Use and Access) Act.
summary: This report discusses the use of copyrighted materials in the development of artificial intelligence systems, as mandated by the Data (Use and Access) Act. It appears to be an update on how AI systems are being developed using copyrighted works, possibly highlighting recent progress or key findings.

title: The regulation of artificial intelligence as a medical device: government response to the RHC
description: Government response to the recommendations made by the Regulatory Horizons Council (RHC) on the regulation of AI as a medical device.
summary: The government is creating regulations for the use of artificial intelligence in medical devices in response to a council's recommendations. The goal is to ensure AI medical devices are safe and effective for use in healthcare settings.

title: ​​Ofqual’s approach to regulating the use of artificial intelligence in the qualifications sector​
description: Published in response to a request from the Secretary of State for Education and the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology.
summary: This document outlines Ofqual's approach to regulating artificial intelligence in qualifications. It aims to provide guidance on how AI will be used in the sector under Ofqual's regulation.

title: Government response to the House of Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence
description: Government response to the Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence report ‘AI in the UK: No Room for Complacency’.
summary: The UK government has responded to a report from the House of Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence, outlining its plans to address the concerns and recommendations made in the report. The government's response aims to ensure the safe and responsible development of artificial intelligence in the UK, following the committee's warnings of complacency.

title: AI and Public Standards – Terms of Reference
description: Terms of Reference and FAQs for the Committee's review into artificial intelligence and public standards.
summary: This document outlines the terms of reference and frequently asked questions for a committee's review of the intersection between artificial intelligence and public standards. The committee will examine how AI aligns with or impacts public standards, such as accountability and transparency.

title: CSPL submission to Science and Technology Committee inquiry on Governance of AI
description: CSPL has submitted written evidence to the Science and Technology Committee's inquiry on Governance of Artificial Intelligence.
summary: The Cabinet Office's Committee on Standards in Public Life (CSPL) has provided written input to a government inquiry about regulating Artificial Intelligence. This submission aims to inform the governance of AI in public life.
